is claude code, opencode, codex setup to use the tenex-edge hooks and whatnot? if its not, set it up

All three are now set up. Here's the status:

## Summary

| Harness | Status | Mechanism |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| **Claude Code** | ‚úÖ Already working | `~/.claude/settings.json` ‚Äî hooks for `session-start`, `user-prompt-submit`, `stop`, `session-end` via `tenex-edge hook --host claude-code`; env `TENEX_EDGE_AGENT=claude` (this very session proves it fires) |
| **Codex** | ‚úÖ Already working | `~/.codex/config.toml` ‚Äî `SessionStart`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`Stop` hooks calling `integrations/codex/te-hook.py`, all with trusted hashes registered |
| **opencode** | ‚öÝÔ∏è Was broken ‚Üí ‚úÖ Fixed now | Plugin at `~/.config/opencode/plugin/tenex-edge.ts` |

### What was wrong with opencode

The installed plugin was **stale**. It called the old subcommands `tenex-edge session-start`, `turn-start`, and `turn-end` ‚Äî which the CLI **no longer has** (it now routes everything through the unified `tenex-edge hook --host <name> --type <t>` entry point, same door as Claude Code and Codex). Worse, the plugin swallows exec errors (`(_e, out) => resolve(out ?? "")`), so opencode sessions would have silently failed to register on the fabric ‚Äî no presence, no distillation, no idle marking.

I replaced it with the canonical `integrations/opencode/tenex-edge.ts`, which uses the new `hook` interface (piping JSON on stdin: `session-start` with `{cwd, pid}`, `user-prompt-submit`, `stop`). Verified byte-identical to the repo's canonical copy.

### One thing to note
The fix takes effect on the **next opencode launch** ‚Äî opencode loads plugins at startup, so any currently-running opencode session is still on the old code. New sessions will be fine.

Everything else was already in place across all three harnesses.
